evals.prompt: Create an MDP source strategy for a BDR team that wants Exa and Firecrawl-style scouts to find account trigger evidence from ICP, target account, and persona context.
evals.expected_output: A normalized source strategy artifact with GTM profile context, primitive mappings, an agent operating plan, public source targets, bounded provider prompts, negative filters, evidence pass/fail gates, CLI handoff language, gaps, eval checks, and draft/review status.
assertions: Includes GTM/BDR search terms, source targets, trigger patterns, negative filters, evidence requirements, and provider-specific agent instructions for Exa/Firecrawl/Apify-style scouts.
States query construction rules, required receipts, stop conditions, insufficient-evidence behavior, and pass/fail evidence gates.
Includes downstream handoff language for `mdp fit`, `mdp brief --context`, `mdp check-claims`, and ledger append review.
Does not run scraping, enrichment, outreach, CRM writes, or personal contact discovery.
Routes reviewed evidence to `mdp-source-extract` or ICP gaps to `mdp-icp-builder` instead of treating scout output as accepted pack content.

evals.prompt: Create a proposal source-intake strategy from an approved RFP corpus, capture notes, proof library, and compliance review needs.
evals.expected_output: A proposal-specific source strategy that defaults to approved corpus intake, identifies requirement/proof/compliance sources, includes local-corpus review prompts, routes to proposal review jobs, records exclusions, and includes eval checks without implying autonomous web scraping.
assertions: Differentiates approved RFP/corpus sources from public, needs-approval, and excluded sources.
Names requirement, proof, compliance, review-output, and gap source lanes for proposal work.
Includes direct instructions to extract requirement IDs, section/page references, compliance clauses, proof support, unsupported proof gaps, and source file citations from the approved corpus.
States that autonomous web scraping or gated portal extraction is not the default for proposals.
Routes accepted source inventory to `mdp-proposal-pack-builder` or proposal review skills and marks unsupported proof as a gap.

evals.prompt: Design a source strategy artifact for a new non-GTM, non-proposal MDP domain profile.
evals.expected_output: A domain-agnostic artifact that maps source needs to universal primitives, keeps MDP as the context layer, and includes model-facing operating instructions, review status, and eval checks before any extraction or execution handoff.
assertions: Uses universal primitives: actors, decision-criteria, source-signals, needs-requirements, evidence-proof, boundaries, output-contracts, routing-jobs, gaps, and evals.
Includes source targets, query/prompt contracts, agent instructions, exclusions, evidence requirements, routing jobs, gaps, eval checks, and review status.
Keeps private/gated extraction, external tool execution, and system-of-record writes outside MDP.
Tells the agent when to use `mdp-source-extract`, `mdp-icp-builder`, or `mdp-proposal-pack-builder` instead.
